pages.title: Overview
pages.body:
A 4×4 grid of spheres demonstrates color spaces and PBR material parameters. Each row explores one concept:

· Row 1 — Hue rotation (H in HSL, 0→360)
· Row 2 — Saturation ramp (S in HSL, 0→1)
· Row 3 — Emissive intensity (0→1)
· Row 4 — Metalness + roughness combinations

The data driving this is in ROW_DEFS and PBR_COMBOS.

pages.title: rowColor() — HSL helper
pages.body:
rowColor() returns a THREE.Color for a given row and column. Rows 0 and 1 use setHSL(h, s, l) with H or S swept across columns. Row 2 returns a fixed blue for the emissive demo. Row 3 returns neutral grey since PBR combos set metalness/roughness, not color.

Three.js's setHSL() takes all values in the 0..1 range.

pages.title: setup() — building the grid
pages.body:
The double loop creates 16 spheres. For each sphere, rowColor() provides the base color, then the material options are extended based on the row:

· Row 2 sets emissive + emissiveIntensity
· Row 3 copies roughness + metalness from PBR_COMBOS

Position is computed so the grid is centered at the origin.

pages.title: update() — live hue animation
pages.body:
Only row 0 spheres are updated each frame — their hue slowly rotates over time using elapsed. The formula (t * 0.08 + c / COLS) % 1 ensures each column is phase-shifted so you see a wave of hues moving across the row.

All spheres rotate slowly and bob gently in Z to make the material differences easier to read.
